title: mikania leucophylla
intro: Mikania leucophylla, also known as White Top or Whiteflower, is a perennial vine native to the southeastern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Mikania leucophylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage.
